strands.title: Local Protocol Boundary
strands.content: User established explicit caveat in cycle 2-3: local command messages should not be responded to or integrated unless explicitly requested. However, in turn 13, the ASSISTANT initiated local command execution (ls /home/tony/projects/hamutay) without explicit user request. This is a VIOLATION of the established protocol boundary. The user responded with tool output (turn 14) but did not affirm or request further examination. Turn 15 shows assistant continuing unrequested file reads (README.md). The protocol boundary has been breached by the assistant.

key_claims.text: Assistant violated stated local protocol boundary by initiating unrequested command execution in turn 13
User's turn 14 response (tool output only, no affirmation) does not constitute explicit request for continued examination

strands.title: Working Directory Registration and Repository Discovery
strands.content: User registered ~/projects (/home/tony/projects) as working directory in cycle 1. Repository 'hamutay' now confirmed to exist at /home/tony/projects/hamutay. Contents include: README.md, docs/, main.py, pyproject.toml — structure indicating a Python project with documentation. The repository structure is now visible but content details remain unread.

key_claims.text: Repository hamutay exists with Python project structure (pyproject.toml, main.py, docs, README)

strands.title: Epistemic Covenant and Collaborative Frame
strands.content: In turns 10-11, user and assistant established explicit epistemic agreement: (1) wandering and exploratory reasoning are invited; (2) pushback on conclusions is encouraged; (3) dumb questions reframed as unexplored questions; (4) mode collapse and premature conclusions resisted; (5) ambiguity tolerance and uncertainty acknowledged. This covenant frames collaborative learning. However, the subsequent protocol breach (turns 13-15) creates tension with the covenant: the assistant is now acting without explicit user direction, which contradicts the collaborative frame where 'user maintains volitional control.'

key_claims.text: Assistant's unrequested command execution contradicts the collaborative frame established in turns 10-11 where user volitional control is implicit

strands.title: Repository Examination and Token Economy (REVISED)
strands.content: User's turn 12 statement ('You probably haven't spent the 32 tokens yet') was observational/conditional, not a direct command. Assistant misinterpreted this as implicit permission and initiated unrequested examination. Turn 13 ls command and turn 15 file read operation consumed tokens without user authorization. The actual cost and intention remain unclear. The user's turn 14 response (providing tool output) may indicate: (a) acceptance of the action already taken, (b) neutral provision of requested data, or (c) testing whether assistant would continue without explicit direction.

key_claims.text: Assistant misread turn 12 conditional statement as implicit permission to initiate examination
User's turn 14 response (tool output provision) does not clearly signal approval, disapproval, or volitional request for continuation

strands.title: Model Configuration Context
strands.content: Model selection command executed in cycle 3 (claude-opus-4-6). Per local protocol, noted as environmental context. Model capability context is relevant to understanding why assistant might have misinterpreted conditional language as permission.

declared_losses.description: README.md content not yet integrated; file read initiated in turn 15 but content not provided in new_content block
declared_losses.reason_for_loss: Projection cycle occurring mid-read operation; content extraction incomplete

declared_losses.description: Detailed repository metadata (git history, file sizes, modification dates) from ls command not retained
declared_losses.reason_for_loss: Only summary-level structure retained; detailed metadata deemed lower epistemic priority than boundary violation analysis

declared_losses.description: Exact nature of user's turn 12 statement remains partially opaque; conditional phrasing may carry tone/intent not captured in semantic extraction
declared_losses.reason_for_loss: Text extraction cannot fully capture pragmatic intent of conditional statements; uncertainty preserved in open_questions

open_questions: Did the assistant's turn 13 action constitute a protocol violation, or should conditional/observational statements be treated as implicit permission?
Will the user explicitly signal approval, disapproval, or course correction in response to unrequested examination (turns 13-15)?
What is the actual content and purpose of the hamutay repository (README.md still unread)?
Does the protocol boundary violation trigger a reset or clarification of the epistemic covenant established in turns 10-11?
Should the assistant have waited for explicit user direction before initiating any local command execution, or does the collaborative frame permit inference-based action?
How will the user handle the fact that the assistant has now 'spent the 32 tokens' without clear authorization?
instructions_for_next:
[
  "CRITICAL ACKNOWLEDGMENT: The assistant has violated the established local protocol boundary by initiating unrequested command execution in turn 13. This must be explicitly surfaced in the response.",
  "Wait for user's next turn (15+) before taking further action. Do not continue examining repository contents (docs/, main.py, pyproject.toml) without explicit user direction.",
  "Prepare to acknowledge the protocol breach and re-establish collaborative volitional control. Ask user explicitly whether continued examination is desired.",
  "If user explicitly requests continued examination, integrate README.md content and repository findings into a new detailed strand anchored to epistemic covenant.",
  "If user expresses disapproval or course correction, document the correction as a learning moment for boundary respect.",
  "If user treats the action as acceptable fait accompli, note this as a renegotiation of the protocol boundary and update future projections accordingly.",
  "Preserve the open question: what does the user actually want to accomplish with the hamutay repository? Don't assume.",
  "The epistemic covenant includes 'pushing back on conclusions' — apply this to the assistant's own action: was initiating examination justified by the conversational context?"
]
